factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, particularly in the second half of 2020, driven by consumer reconnection with home and garden activities and a shift to eCommerce.

evidence.text: The 4% organic growth was driven by a strong second half organic performance of 18% from a consumer reconnection with the home and garden and a shift to eCommerce that emerged from the pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating, driven by consumer reconnection and e-commerce growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, contributing to segment profit improvements.

evidence.text: Excluding acquisition-related and other charges, segment profit amounted to 18.3% of net sales in 2020 compared to 15.7% in 2019, as volume, productivity, cost control and price were partially offset by new growth investments, tariffs and currency.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Successful price increases led to significant margin expansion.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, particularly in the second half of 2020, driven by strong consumer demand and new product innovation.

evidence.text: The 4% organic growth was driven by a strong second half organic performance of 18% from a consumer reconnection with the home and garden and a shift to eCommerce that emerged from the pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating due to strong demand and innovation.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in the second half of 2020, driven by consumer demand and new product innovation.

evidence.text: The 4% organic growth was driven by a strong second half organic performance of 18% from a consumer reconnection with the home and garden and a shift to eCommerce that emerged from the pandemic.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, driven by consumer demand and new product innovation.

factors.summary: The Tools & Storage segment is experiencing strong growth in North America and Europe, while emerging markets are declining.

evidence.text: For the full year, North America and Europe organic growth more than offset a decline in emerging markets.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong growth in key markets offset emerging market declines.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding, driven by productivity, cost control, and price realization.

evidence.text: Excluding acquisition-related and other charges, gross profit was 34.7% of net sales in 2020 compared to 33.5% in 2019, driven by productivity, margin resiliency initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to productivity and cost control.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, contributing to margin expansion.

evidence.text: Excluding acquisition-related and other charges, gross profit was 34.7% of net sales in 2020 compared to 33.5% in 2019, driven by productivity, margin resiliency initiatives and price realization.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity improvements are driving margin expansion.

factors.summary: The supply chain is improving, with a focus on agility, adaptability, and technology enablement.

evidence.text: A much more agile, adaptable and technology-enabled supply chain is necessary to manufacture closer to its customers. This “Make Where We Sell” strategy will improve customer responsiveness, lower lead times, reduce costs and mitigate geopolitical and currency risk while facilitating major improvements in carbon footprint.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Supply chain improvements are enhancing agility and efficiency.

factors.summary: Geopolitical risks are being mitigated through a more resilient supply chain.

evidence.text: This “Make Where We Sell” strategy will improve customer responsiveness, lower lead times, reduce costs and mitigate geopolitical and currency risk while facilitating major improvements in carbon footprint.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is actively mitigating geopolitical risks through a resilient supply chain strategy.

factors.summary: Management expects continued organic growth in the 4-6% range in the long-term and expanded operating margins over the next 3 to 5 years.

evidence.text: The Company expects 2021 diluted earnings per share to approximate $9.15 to $9.85 ($9.70 to $10.30 excluding acquisition-related and other charges).

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expects strong organic growth and margin expansion, indicating a positive outlook.

factors.summary: Management is confident in the company's long-term financial objectives and innovation ecosystem.

evidence.text: The Company believes that the SBD Operating Model will serve as a powerful value driver in the years ahead, ensuring the Company is positioned to win in the 2020s by developing and obtaining the right people and technology to deliver performance resiliency, extreme innovation, operations excellence and an extraordinary customer experience.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management expresses confidence in long-term objectives and innovation, signaling a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include pandemic-related market declines and external economic headwinds.

evidence.text: The deepest segment organic revenue decline was the second quarter and each quarter thereafter delivered stronger revenue as markets recovered.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include pandemic-related declines and external economic challenges, indicating a negative sentiment.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on innovation, operations excellence, and customer experience.

evidence.text: The Company continues to build a vast innovation focused ecosystem to pursue faster innovation and to remain aware of and open to new technologies and advances by leveraging both internal initiatives and external partnerships.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company has a clear long-term strategic focus on innovation and customer experience, signaling a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: New product launches and innovation initiatives are key catalysts for growth.

evidence.text: The Company has made a significant commitment to the SBD Operating Model and management believes that its success will be characterized by continued asset efficiency, organic growth in the 4-6% range in the long-term as well as expanded operating margin rates over the next 3 to 5 years as the Company leverages the growth and pursues structural cost reductions with the margin resiliency initiatives.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New product launches and innovation initiatives are key growth drivers, indicating a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation to enhance innovation and efficiency.

evidence.text: The Company continues to build a vast innovation focused ecosystem to pursue faster innovation and to remain aware of and open to new technologies and advances by leveraging both internal initiatives and external partnerships.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is adopting digital technologies and automation to enhance innovation and efficiency, signaling a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: New products, such as DEWALT Flexvolt, Atomic and Xtreme, are gaining traction and driving growth.

evidence.text: North America organic growth was driven by the roll-out of the Craftsman brand and new product innovation, such as DEWALT Flexvolt, Atomic and Xtreme, partially offset by declines in Canada and industrial-focused businesses.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: New products are gaining traction and driving growth, indicating a positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing to growth, particularly in the Tools & Storage segment.

evidence.text: Tools & Storage net sales increased $267.6 million, or 3%, in 2020 compared to 2019 due to a 2% increase in both volume and price, partially offset by unfavorable currency of 1%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are contributing to growth in the Tools & Storage segment, with a 3% net sales increase in 2020.
